title: centaurea × pailleri
intro: Centaurea × pailleri, also known as the Pailler's cornflower or Pailler's knapweed, is a plant species in the aster family, derived from the cross between Centaurea montana and Centaurea scabiosa. This plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Centaurea × pailleri include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Centaurea × pailleri can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
